title: Three-breath reset before opening bills
subtitle: A two-minute breath practice for arriving at bills from a settled state rather than a braced one.
excerpt: A two-minute breath practice for the moment before opening a bill, an email statement, or the banking app. Three deliberate breaths, one to arrive, one to release the charge, one to open with. Use before any financial task that triggers dread rather than neutrality.
sourceNotes: Deliberate breath as a pre-task settling practice is documented across mindfulness traditions (Kabat-Zinn's MBSR, 1979 onward) and evidence-based stress physiology. The money-anxiety application is adapted from Day 7 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Invite peace around paying bills."

content.text: A two-minute breath practice for the moment before opening a bill or the banking app. Three deliberate breaths, one to arrive, one to release the charge, one to open with. The practice doesn't change the number in the bill; it changes the state you read the number from.

Before touching the bill or the phone or the keyboard. Both hands still. In through the nose for a count of four. Out through the mouth for a count of six. The out-breath is longer. This breath says: I am here, and I am not in a hurry.

In through the nose for a count of four. Then a long, slow out-breath through a slightly open mouth, as long as the breath will go. Let the shoulders drop on the out-breath. This breath releases the charge that has been building since the bill arrived.
In for four. Out for six. At the end of this breath, open the bill or the app. You are already on the other side of the dread.
If three breaths aren't enough to lower the dread, pause and tap before attempting the task.
clears the charge more completely. Come back to the three-breath reset after the tapping round.

Slow breath with a long out-breath as a settling practice shows up across mindfulness traditions. Kabat-Zinn's MBSR is one example, from 1979 on. The money-anxiety use is adapted from Day 7 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
